title: system tests: run test: reenable and fix
body:
Test had incorrectly been disabled for all podman; it
should've been disabled only for podman-remote. Fixed
that, and fixed the problem that was causing failures:
podman-remote is gobbling up stdin (#4095), so no
tests were actually being run at all, or only one.
Fixed by redirecting input on the run_podman invocation.
Added, as backup, a confirmation mechanism to ensure
that all expected tests are being run.

Test remains disabled for podman-remote due to #4096

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
UPDATE: force-pushed a better idea - instead of blanket-disabling this test for podman-remote, enable it, but disable the output check. This at least lets us check exit status of podman-remote.
